preamble: The transformation of the voiced alveolar nasal [n] into a voiced uvular nasal [ɴ]. This shift typically occurs at the end of an utterance or before a pause, representing a place shift toward the back of the vocal tract.

examples.note: The mora nasal is realized as a uvular nasal word-finally or before a pause.

sources: Vance, T. J. (2008). The Sounds of Japanese.
